text.title: python-pytest-mocking Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for python-pytest-mocking as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using mocker.Mock() (not AsyncMock) for an async callable in an async test.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A plain Mock() returns a non-awaitable object. The test passes because await mock() evaluates to Mock.__await__() returning the mock itself — silently asserting nothing about the async call.

failure-mode.description: Mocking every collaborator of the unit under test including internal validation, duplicate checking, persistence, and notification — leaving the test only verifying that the mocks were called.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A test with more mock setup than real assertion tests only the mock configuration, not the production code path. If the service's logic is wrong, this test passes. Mock only external dependencies (network, disk, time); let the production code run for everything inside the service boundary.
